questionHtml:
An aqueous lead(II) ion reacts with three ethane-1,2-diamine molecules to form an octahedral chelate ion.

Outline why the chelate ion is more stable than the reactants.

meta.title: An aqueous lead(II) ion reacts with three ethane-1,2-diamine molecules to form an octahedral chelate ion. Outline why the chelate ion is more stable than the reactants.
